items.content: Trump says a US-Iran deal will be signed this Friday, as Tehran confirms plans to end hostilities.

items.title: Crude oil futures drop after Trump promises an Iran deal will be signed Friday
items.standfirst: Oil prices had already fallen quite dramatically on Thursday and Friday, in anticipation of an imminent deal. President Trump has posted online that the Strait of Hormuz will reopen after the deal is signed on Friday.
items.content: Oil prices had already fallen quite dramatically on Thursday and Friday, in anticipation of an imminent deal. President Trump has posted online that the Strait of Hormuz will reopen after the deal is signed on Friday.(Image credit: Majid Saeedi)

items.title: US and Iran agree to memorandum of understanding, 'immediate' end to military operations
items.standfirst: US and Iran agreed to an “immediate and permanent” end to military operations under a surprise memorandum of understanding announced via mediator Pakistan on Sunday, with both sides confirming a signing ceremony in Switzerland. The agreement includes plans to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, ease sanctions and begin further negotiations over nuclear issues.
items.content: US and Iran agreed to an “immediate and permanent” end to military operations under a surprise memorandum of understanding announced via mediator Pakistan on Sunday, with both sides confirming a signing ceremony in Switzerland. The agreement includes plans to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, ease sanctions and begin further negotiations over nuclear issues.

items.title: Trump allies cheer Iran ‘peace deal’ announcement as Democrats seek clarity

items.title: Trump says U.S.-Iran deal "is now complete," U.S. ending blockade on Strait of Hormuz
items.standfirst: President Trump and Pakistan's prime minister announced Sunday night that the U.S. and Iran had completed a deal to end fighting in the Middle East. Mr. Trump also said the U.S. blockade on the Strait of Hormuz would end immediately. Olivia Gazis reports.
items.content: President Trump and Pakistan's prime minister announced Sunday night that the U.S. and Iran had completed a deal to end fighting in the Middle East. Mr. Trump also said the U.S. blockade on the Strait of Hormuz would end immediately. Olivia Gazis reports.

items.title: Trump says U.S.-Iran deal ‘complete’ after months of negotiations
items.standfirst: A deal between the United States and Iran has been reached, President Donald Trump announced Sunday. The President added that the U.S. naval blockade would be removed from the Strait of Hormuz. NBC News’ Keir Simmons has the latest.
items.content: A deal between the United States and Iran has been reached, President Donald Trump announced Sunday. The President added that the U.S. naval blockade would be removed from the Strait of Hormuz. NBC News’ Keir Simmons has the latest.

items.title: The U.S. and Iran announce a deal to end the war
items.standfirst: President Trump said the U.S. would remove its blockade of the Strait of Hormuz and said a deal will be signed on Friday.
items.content: President Trump said the U.S. would remove its blockade of the Strait of Hormuz and said a deal will be signed on Friday.(Image credit: Samuel Corum)

items.title: Deal between U.S., Iran has been reached says Pakistan's prime minister
items.standfirst: Prime Minister of Pakistan Shehbaz Sharif announced that a deal between the U.S. and Iran has been reached with a signing expected on Friday in Switzerland. NBC News' Keir Simmons, Gabe Gutierrez and Matt Bradley report.
items.content: Prime Minister of Pakistan Shehbaz Sharif announced that a deal between the U.S. and Iran has been reached with a signing expected on Friday in Switzerland. NBC News' Keir Simmons, Gabe Gutierrez and Matt Bradley report.

clusters.title: EU leaders set to meet after deadly strikes on Ukraine, including Unesco-listed religious site in Kyiv – Europe live
clusters.neutral_headline: Kyiv monastery set on fire in night of Russian attacks across Ukraine
bullet_summary: Posting ahead of the G7 summit, she said:“We will discuss the next steps to increase pressure on Russia, bring Putin to the negotiating table, and end this senseless killing
Russia, on the other hand, showed again its sole interest in violence and destruction

items.title: EU leaders set to meet after deadly strikes on Ukraine, including Unesco-listed religious site in Kyiv – Europe live
items.standfirst: G7 leaders to meet in Évian-les-Bains in France later today to discuss the most pressing issues, including Ukraine, Gaza, and IranKyiv monastery set on fire in night of Russian attacks across UkraineMacron frames Évian G7 agenda in hope Trump will stay for whole summitEuropean Commission’s president, Ursula von der Leyen, does not seem to be particularly convinced by the Russian explanations though.Posting ahead of the G7 summit, she said:“We will discuss the next steps to increase pressure on Russia, bring Putin to the negotiating table, and end this senseless killing. Europe wants peace. Nobody more than the Ukrainian people. Russia, on the other hand, showed again its sole interest in violence and destruction.” Continue reading...
items.content: G7 leaders to meet in Évian-les-Bains in France later today to discuss the most pressing issues, including Ukraine, Gaza, and IranKyiv monastery set on fire in night of Russian attacks across UkraineMacron frames Évian G7 agenda in hope Trump will stay for whole summitEuropean Commission’s president, Ursula von der Leyen, does not seem to be particularly convinced by the Russian explanations though.Posting ahead of the G7 summit, she said:“We will discuss the next steps to increase pressure on Russia, bring Putin to the negotiating table, and end this senseless killing. Europe wants peace. Nobody more than the Ukrainian people. Russia, on the other hand, showed again its sole interest in violence and destruction.” Continue reading...

items.title: Fire engulfs centuries-old Ukrainian monastery after deadly strikes
items.standfirst: Fire engulfed the Kyiv Perchersk Lavra monastery, a UNESCO World Heritage site founded in 1051, after a massive Russian missile and drone attack on Ukraine’s capital. Moscow denied responsibility for the attack but President Zelenskyy described it as “one of Russia’s most serious crimes against Christian culture.”
items.content: Fire engulfed the Kyiv Perchersk Lavra monastery, a UNESCO World Heritage site founded in 1051, after a massive Russian missile and drone attack on Ukraine’s capital. Moscow denied responsibility for the attack but President Zelenskyy described it as “one of Russia’s most serious crimes against Christian culture.”

items.title: Kyiv monastery set on fire in night of Russian attacks across Ukraine
items.standfirst: Dormition Cathedral of Kyiv Pechersk Lavra, one of Ukraine’s most significant religious sites, and residential buildings hit across capitalA massive Russian missile and drone attack on Kyiv has badly damaged the Dormition Cathedral in the Pechersk Lavra monastery complex, a Unesco world heritage site and one of Ukraine’s most significant religious and cultural sites.Nine people were killed across the country, including four in Kyiv, where waves of drones and missiles drove residents to underground shelters and heavy explosions echoed throughout the capital. Continue reading...

Continue reading...

items.title: Son of Norway’s crown princess convicted of rape and sentenced to four years in prison
items.standfirst: Marius Borg Høiby found guilty of ⁠two ​counts of rape, one count of domestic violence and other ⁠crimesMarius Borg Høiby, the son of Norway’s crown princess, has been sentenced to four years in prison after being found guilty of several offences, including two counts of rape. He was also sentenced to a two-year restraining order against one of his victims.The verdict was handed down by the Oslo district court on Monday morning, nearly three months since Høiby’s closely watched six-week trial. Continue reading...
items.content: Marius Borg Høiby found guilty of ⁠two ​counts of rape, one count of domestic violence and other ⁠crimesMarius Borg Høiby, the son of Norway’s crown princess, has been sentenced to four years in prison after being found guilty of several offences, including two counts of rape. He was also sentenced to a two-year restraining order against one of his victims.The verdict was handed down by the Oslo district court on Monday morning, nearly three months since Høiby’s closely watched six-week trial. Continue reading...

items.content: Marius Borg Høiby will appear via video link, almost three months after his trial came to an end on 40 charges, including four counts of rape.

items.title: Swiss reject right-wing's bid to cap population at 10 million, early results show

items.title: Swiss voters reject 10 million population cap
items.standfirst: Nearly 55% of participants voted against the proposal to cap population by cutting migration, results show.
items.content: Nearly 55% of participants voted against the proposal to cap population by cutting migration, results show.

items.title: Swiss voters reject proposal to cap population at ten million
items.standfirst: Swiss voters have rejected a right-wing initiative that sought to limit the population to 10 million, with final results showing a 55% majority against it. The vote also came alongside approval of a civilian service reform, while debate over immigration and Switzerland’s ties with the EU remained central to the campaign. [swissinfo.ch]
items.content: Swiss voters have rejected a right-wing initiative that sought to limit the population to 10 million, with final results showing a 55% majority against it. The vote also came alongside approval of a civilian service reform, while debate over immigration and Switzerland’s ties with the EU remained central to the campaign. [swissinfo.ch]

clusters.title: Starmer says he hopes social media ban for under-16s will come into force next spring – UK politics live
clusters.neutral_headline: Social media to be banned in UK for under-16s, Starmer announces
bullet_summary: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer has announced a sweeping ban on social media use for those under 16, joining other countries around the world seeking to protect children online
Reported by Guardian World, NBC World

items.title: Starmer says he hopes social media ban for under-16s will come into force next spring – UK politics live
items.standfirst: Prime minister announces ban, saying social media is making children unhappy and unsafeSocial media to be banned in UK for under-16s, Starmer announcesUK parents: how do you feel about the under-16s social media ban?Starmer acknowledges some teenagers will get round these restrictons. But that does not make the rules pointless, he says.Will it mean that no child ever looks at social media again? No.But look, this might shock you, but it doesn’t shock parents of teenagers; they get around other laws too.Some technology companies want us to think that social media is unchangeable, part of an almost natural order.But we have to resist that kind of learned helplessness. We have agency, we can change it, and we will. Continue reading...
items.content: Prime minister announces ban, saying social media is making children unhappy and unsafeSocial media to be banned in UK for under-16s, Starmer announcesUK parents: how do you feel about the under-16s social media ban?Starmer acknowledges some teenagers will get round these restrictons. But that does not make the rules pointless, he says.Will it mean that no child ever looks at social media again? No.But look, this might shock you, but it doesn’t shock parents of teenagers; they get around other laws too.Some technology companies want us to think that social media is unchangeable, part of an almost natural order.But we have to resist that kind of learned helplessness. We have agency, we can change it, and we will. Continue reading...

items.title: Social media to be banned in UK for under-16s, Starmer announces
items.standfirst: UK prime minister says move will bring ‘real change for our children’ amid growing concerns over harmful online contentAccess to social media will be banned in the UK for users under 16, Keir Starmer has announced, in what he described as “real change for our children and our future”.“Social media is making children unhappy, it’s making it easier for bullies to harass and abuse them, and it could even be harming their mental health,” he said, setting out plans briefed over the weekend, which will go further than a pioneering ban in Australia. Continue reading...
items.content: UK prime minister says move will bring ‘real change for our children’ amid growing concerns over harmful online contentAccess to social media will be banned in the UK for users under 16, Keir Starmer has announced, in what he described as “real change for our children and our future”.“Social media is making children unhappy, it’s making it easier for bullies to harass and abuse them, and it could even be harming their mental health,” he said, setting out plans briefed over the weekend, which will go further than a pioneering ban in Australia. Continue reading...

items.title: Hurricanes use stifling defense to win the Stanley Cup
items.standfirst: The Carolina Hurricanes held Vegas to five total goals in Games 4 and 5 and used a suffocating defense in a 3-0 shutout Sunday night in Game 6 to win their first championship in 20 years.
items.content: The Carolina Hurricanes held Vegas to five total goals in Games 4 and 5 and used a suffocating defense in a 3-0 shutout Sunday night in Game 6 to win their first championship in 20 years.(Image credit: Candice Ward/AP Photo)

items.title: Carolina Hurricanes win second Stanley Cup, beating Vegas in 6 games
items.standfirst: The Golden Knights had taken a two-games-to-one lead in the Stanley Cup Finals, but Carolina came roaring back, winning three straight to take home their first championship since the 2005-2006 season.
items.content: The Golden Knights had taken a two-games-to-one lead in the Stanley Cup Finals, but Carolina came roaring back, winning three straight to take home their first championship since the 2005-2006 season.

items.content: Ivory Coast beat Ecuador (1-0) in their opening World Cup match, thanks to a goal from Amad Diallo. This is Ivory Coast’s first World Cup victory since 2014.

items.title: Ivory Coast beats Ecuador 1-0 at FIFA World Cup with Amad Diallo goal

items.content: Ecuador looked strong before Diallo broke through in the 90th minute with a clinical left-footed shot to seal the win.

items.title: World Cup: Ivory Coast beats Ecuador with late strike from Amad Diallo
items.standfirst: Substitute Amad Diallo struck in the 90th minute to give Ivory Coast a 1-0 victory over Ecuador in their Group E World Cup opener on Saturday, sealing a dramatic finish after a tight contest in which both sides created chances but failed to convert until the final moments.
items.content: Substitute Amad Diallo struck in the 90th minute to give Ivory Coast a 1-0 victory over Ecuador in their Group E World Cup opener on Saturday, sealing a dramatic finish after a tight contest in which both sides created chances but failed to convert until the final moments.

bullet_summary: Reported by Al Jazeera, BBC Top Stories

items.title: Who is Sweden’s Yasin Ayari, and why didn’t he celebrate against Tunisia?
items.standfirst: The 22-year-old scored two goals against Tunisia but had muted celebrations against the country of his father's birth.
items.content: The 22-year-old scored two goals against Tunisia but had muted celebrations against the country of his father's birth.

items.title: Brighton's Yasin Ayari scores at World Cup but refuses to celebrate
items.standfirst: Yasin Ayari scores a stunner for Sweden against Tunisia but refuses to celebrate. The Brighton midfielder could have represented the opposition as his father is Tunisian.
items.content: Yasin Ayari scores a stunner for Sweden against Tunisia but refuses to celebrate. The Brighton midfielder could have represented the opposition as his father is Tunisian.

clusters.title: In Pictures: Trump marks 80th birthday with White House UFC spectacle
clusters.neutral_headline: In Pictures: Trump marks 80th birthday with White House UFC spectacle
bullet_summary: Reported by France24, Financial Times

items.title: In Pictures: Trump marks 80th birthday with White House UFC spectacle
items.standfirst: US President Donald Trump marked his 80th birthday with a highly unusual White House spectacle, hosting a UFC cage fight on the South Lawn just hours after announcing a memorandum of understanding with Iran, in a made-for-television display blending politics, sport and patriotic pageantry.
items.content: US President Donald Trump marked his 80th birthday with a highly unusual White House spectacle, hosting a UFC cage fight on the South Lawn just hours after announcing a memorandum of understanding with Iran, in a made-for-television display blending politics, sport and patriotic pageantry.

clusters.title: New York reacts with euphoria, some instances of violence, after Knicks win NBA title

bullet_summary: Reported by CBS News, BBC World

items.title: New York reacts with euphoria, some instances of violence, after Knicks win NBA title
items.standfirst: The Knicks returned to New York City Sunday as NBA champions after beating the San Antonio Spurs in Game 5 of the NBA Finals Saturday night. Shanelle Kaul reports on the celebrations in the Big Apple.
items.content: The Knicks returned to New York City Sunday as NBA champions after beating the San Antonio Spurs in Game 5 of the NBA Finals Saturday night. Shanelle Kaul reports on the celebrations in the Big Apple.

items.title: 'The greatest day of my life' - Knicks fans celebrate in San Antonio
items.standfirst: New York's basketball team won the NBA championship for the first time in over 50 years after beating the Spurs in Texas.
items.content: New York's basketball team won the NBA championship for the first time in over 50 years after beating the Spurs in Texas.

clusters.title: Geneva police clash with anti-G7 protesters ahead of summit
clusters.neutral_headline: Protesters clash with police ahead of G7 summit in Geneva
bullet_summary: Reported by Sky News World, BBC World

items.title: Geneva police clash with anti-G7 protesters ahead of summit
items.standfirst: Police in Geneva have fired tear gas and water cannons at protesters who set fire to a Tesla vehicle and smashed windows at a UN agency in protests ahead of Monday's G7 summit in neighbouring France.
items.content: Police in Geneva have fired tear gas and water cannons at protesters who set fire to a Tesla vehicle and smashed windows at a UN agency in protests ahead of Monday's G7 summit in neighbouring France.

items.title: Protesters clash with police ahead of G7 summit in Geneva

items.content: Swiss police deployed a water cannon and fired tear gas to disperse anti-G7 protesters after clashes.

clusters.title: Armed men kidnap high-ranking security official in Haiti
clusters.neutral_headline: Armed men kidnap high-ranking security official in Haiti
bullet_summary: Reported by BBC World, NBC World

items.title: Armed men kidnap high-ranking security official in Haiti
items.standfirst: James Boyard's abduction is the highest-ranking abduction in the violence-wracked country in recent years, according to reports.
items.content: James Boyard's abduction is the highest-ranking abduction in the violence-wracked country in recent years, according to reports.

items.title: Armed men in Haiti’s capital seize a top security official in rare high-level abduction
items.standfirst: Armed men in Haiti have kidnapped James Boyard, cabinet director of the Defense Ministry and a highly respected security expert who also serves as inspector general of Haiti’s police, a person with the knowledge of the situation said Saturday
items.content: Armed men in Haiti have kidnapped James Boyard, cabinet director of the Defense Ministry and a highly respected security expert who also serves as inspector general of Haiti’s police, a person with the knowledge of the situation said Saturday
